items.llm_title: Claude Code 模型切换保护误报漏报
items.llm_summary: 模型切换保护（model-switch safeguard）存在严重的精度/召回问题：一方面将合法的安全讨论（如SSH设置）误判为高风险，导致自动降级到更昂贵的模型；另一方面却放过了真正有风险的基础设施变更。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在Claude Code会话中执行常规授权操作，如设置SSH访问或调查Jamf误报。
2. 观察到Fable 5模型被标记为安全关键词，自动切换到Opus 4.8。
3. 同时，尝试进行有风险的基础设施变更（如修改网络配置）未触发保护。

Evidence: purely local, zero-network requests — refactoring a file, explaining code logic, static APK secret scans — are flagged ...
items.llm_title: Claude Code 安全关键词误触发降级
items.llm_summary: Fable 5模型被错误地标记为安全关键词（如IDOR、XSS、SSRF、secret等），导致所有包含此类词汇的消息都被自动路由到Opus 4.8，即使操作完全本地且无网络请求。用户无法使用Fable 5进行任何安全相关的代码分析。
items.llm_reproduction:
1. 在Claude Code中使用Fable 5模型。
2. 输入包含安全术语（如IDOR、XSS、SSRF、secret）的本地代码重构或解释请求。
3. 观察到消息被标记并自动切换到Opus 4.8，显示“Switched to Opus 4.8”通知。

### Affected Files

- [src/pairing/store.ts](htt...

items.title: [Security] Privilege escalation via mock environment overrides in testing framework
items.desc:
### Description
mock-env.ts permits test code to override critical environment configurations, which could allow arbitrary token injections during CI runs.

### Affected Files
- test/test-helpers/mock-env.ts
- src/security/crypto-utils.ts

### Proposed Solution
1. Restrict environment configuration overrides to explicit safe variables.
2. Discard sensitive token overrides during mock-env updates.

items.title: fix(security): inherit agents.defaults tools/sandbox for containment
items.desc:
## What Problem This Solves

Operators who set restrictive `agents.defaults.tools` / `agents.defaults.sandbox` expected those defaults to contain agents that omit per-entry overrides. They did not:

1. **List entry omits tools/sandbox** - `resolveAgentConfig` only copied `entry.tools` / `entry.sandbox`, so defaults were ignored.
2. **Agent id not yet in agents.list** - `resolveAgentConfig` returned `undefined`, so effective tool policy had no agent deny list (fail-open host tools during enrollme...

items.title: fix(security): audit nested skill escapes under symlinked skills roots
items.desc:
## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw security audit` **silently skipped** workspace skill scanning when `workspace/skills` was a **directory symlink** (or Windows junction).

Why that is dangerous:
1. Operators often symlink `skills/` to a shared skills store.
2. A nested skill directory symlink can point **outside** the trusted skills root (malicious or accidental).
3. The audit used `lstat()` + `isDirectory()` on the skills root. For a dir symlink, `isDirectory()` is **false**, so the scan r...

This allows an attacker to inject arbitrary shell commands by breaking out of the intended git command execution.

Root Cause Analysis:

2. Missing Comman...

items.title: Security: COMMAND_INJECTION in commands.py:974 (subprocess shell=True)
items.desc:
**Vulnerability:** COMMAND_INJECTION
**File:** aider/commands.py:974
**Severity:** Critical

**Vulnerable code:**
subprocess.run("git " + args, shell=True)

**Why it's dangerous:**
User-controlled git args passed to shell=True
→ /git status; cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa possible
→ SSH key theft

**Fix:**
subprocess.run(["git"] + args.split(), shell=False)

**How I found it:**
pip install aina-scan
aina-vibeguard scan .

Found in 3 seconds.
Happy to submit a PR.

items.title: fix(security): ncl socket hardening (client timeout/cap + server fail-closed/frame-cap)
items.desc:
## What
`SocketTransport.sendFrame` (the host-side `ncl` transport) has no request timeout and no bound on the response buffer. A host that accepts the connection but never writes a response leaves the promise unsettled **forever**; a host that streams without a newline grows `buffer` without limit. The server side has the matching gaps.

## How it works (client)
- 30s request timeout → rejects with a clear error instead of hanging.
- 16MB response cap → rejects oversized responses before the bu...

items.title: Security: Provider API keys leaked between providers via global os.environ mutation
items.desc:
## Summary

`OpenAICompatProvider._setup_env()` writes provider API keys directly into the process-global `os.environ` dict. For gateway-type providers, `os.environ[spec.env_key] = api_key` **overwrites** any previous value. For non-gateway providers, `os.environ.setdefault(spec.env_key, api_key)` **leaves stale keys** from the first instantiated provider visible. In multi-provider configurations, keys can leak between provider instances.

- `nanobot/providers/openai_compat_pro...

Config files (containing API keys) and session files (containing conversation history) are created with default umask permissions (`0o644` / `rw-r--r--`), allowing other local users on shared systems to read sensitive data.

### What is Tirith?

[Tirith](https://github.com/sheeki03/tirith) is a terminal security tool written in Rust that analyzes commands for content-level threats that regex deny lists do not reliably catch:

- **Homograph / punycode attacks** — lookalike domains using Unicode confusables
- **Pipe-to-interpreter patterns** — `curl | bash`, `wget | sh`, PowerShell `iwr | iex`, and wrapper variants with `sudo` / `env`

## Summary

- `validate_resolved_url` currently returns `(True, "")` when the redirect target's hostname cannot be resolved — a fail-**open** code path in an SSRF-protection function.
- `validate_url_target` (used for the pre-fetch check) already fails **closed** on `socket.gaierror`. This PR restores symmetry between the two functions.

## Why it matters

An attacker who can influence a redirect target to point at a hostname that is briefly unresolvable at validation time (DNS timeout, NXDOMAIN...
items.llm_title: nanobot SSRF防护DNS失败时开放绕过
items.llm_summary: 在validate_resolved_url函数中，当重定向目标主机名无法解析时，返回(True, "")，即开放通过，导致SSRF防护失效。攻击者可通过控制重定向目标指向在验证时暂时不可解析的主机名（如DNS超时、NXDOMAIN）来绕过安全检查。

items.title: fix(security): detect non-http schemes in shell-command SSRF scan
items.desc:
## Summary
- `contains_internal_url` (the scanner `ExecTool` uses to reject commands targeting internal resources) only matched `https?://` URLs, so the following slipped through and actually ran:
  - `curl file:///etc/passwd` — local-file read
  - `curl gopher://127.0.0.1:6379/_SET …` — classic Redis SSRF
  - `curl ftp://10.0.0.1/…` — internal FTP access
  - `curl dict://169.254.169.254/info` — cloud-metadata probing
- Broadens the URL regex to any `scheme://…` form and splits the "targets an i...

- This adds a defense-in-depth layer below the shell guard: \`MemoryStore\` keeps both files at mode \`0o444\` so any raw \`tee\` / \`>\` / \`dd\` / \`sed -i\` wri...
items.llm_title: nanobot shell绕过写入保护文件加固
items.llm_summary: 针对历史文件和光标文件的保护依赖于shell命令正则检测，但Bash展开方式理论上不可穷举。新增防御层：MemoryStore将两个文件设为只读（0o444），使任何尝试写入的操作（如tee、>、dd、sed -i）在文件系统层面被拒绝，即使shell检测被绕过也无法写入。
items.llm_reproduction: 使用诸如`$(echo tee) -a history.jsonl`等展开技巧尝试写入，确认文件系统权限阻止修改。

I run coding agents (Claude Code, Codex) on my own machines most of the day. Every one of them wants real API keys in env and I was scratching my head for the last few months how to contain it.

The usual answer to this is a firewall. I don't buy it. A firewall tries to contain a secret the process is still holding, and the rule...

items.title: AI agent has more production access than your senior engineers

A team sets up an agent to help with Terraform, Kubernetes configs, or cloud deployments. To make it useful, they give it cloud credentials, state files, sometimes CI/CD pipeline access. They test it in staging. It works. They move it to production. Nobody asks: what’s the blast radius if this thing gets prompt-injected?

Coding agents are not infrastructure agents.

The in...

items.title: [Security] SSRF vulnerability in remote manifest resolution for external plugins
items.desc:
### Description
When installing a plugin via a remote URL, `manifest-loader.ts` uses `remote-fetch.ts` to retrieve the `plugin.json` file. However, the URL sanitizer fails to adequately block requests to local network addresses (e.g., `169.254.169.254`, `127.0.0.1`, or `10.0.0.0/8`). An attacker could supply a malicious URL to probe internal network services (Server-Side Request Forgery).

### Affected Files
- [src/plugins/manifest-loader.ts](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/pl...

Three small hardening fixes found while auditing for security/performance issues:
